summary: Full end-to-end test of the iteration_10 DM + Auto-Join redesign. Backend: 8/8 pytest cases PASS (auto-join new-user→World Vibez + resume, join/leave preserving lastRoomId, DM unread total increment+mark-as-read, conversation delete count, dm-settings defaults & PUT persistence, seeded users smoke). Frontend (Playwright on the public preview URL): login as dmtest1 → auto-join-indicator rendered ✓, navigated into /room/<World Vibez id> ✓, direct-messages-btn visible ✓, dm-unread-dot rendered after a seeded DM ✓, DM modal opens with both tabs ✓, conversation row tapped → options popup with all 4 buttons (view-profile/view-message/report-block/delete) ✓, View Message opens chat view with composer input + send button ✓, Settings tab shows 3 chips + notifications toggle and the Friends chip click was persisted server-side (GET /dm-settings returned allowMessagesFrom='friends').

issues: Inactive tab label ('Settings' when Messages is active) is white on light-blue (#5cc1f2), low contrast. Active tab uses pure white with a yellow underline which reads fine. Consider darkening the inactive tab text or adding higher contrast.
Notifications toggle thumb on the OFF (false) track is white-on-light-grey (#cbd5e1), low visibility. Functional, but easy to miss.

verified_flows.POST /api/rooms/auto-join (new user): PASS — returns roomName='World Vibez', wasResumed=false; second call returns wasResumed=true with same roomId.
verified_flows.Join + Leave preserves lastRoomId: PASS — leave clears currentRoomId but auto-join resumes the same room via lastRoomId.
verified_flows.GET /api/messages/direct/unread/total: PASS — base→+2 after two DMs, then back to base after conversation fetch (mark-as-read).
verified_flows.DELETE /api/messages/direct/conversation: PASS — returns 'deleted':4, subsequent GETs from both sides return []
verified_flows.GET/PUT /api/users/me/dm-settings: PASS — defaults everyone/true; PUT friends+false persists; PUT nobody keeps notifications=false; invalid 'aliens' silently ignored (200 + no change).
verified_flows.Frontend auto-join-indicator (testid): PASS — detected ~150ms after Sign-In click on dmtest1; landed on /room/6a251e92a684769d62955051 (World Vibez).
verified_flows.Frontend dm-unread-dot: PASS — appeared on direct-messages-btn after seeding a DM from dmtest2 → dmtest1.
verified_flows.Frontend DM modal (tabs + conv row + opt: PASS — all listed data-testids present and functional. Settings chip click persisted to backend (allowMessagesFrom='friends').

action_items: (LOW / cosmetic) Improve contrast on inactive tab label in DM modal top bar (light-blue background).
(LOW) Notification toggle thumb visibility when OFF.
(LOW) Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents.' — pre-existing from iteration_9, still emitted; safe to ignore but worth migrating.

(NOTE) The auto-join indicator first renders 'Finding your room…' for ~50–200ms before swapping to 'Joining <RoomName>…'. The testID auto-join-indicator is the same in both states, so spec is met. If the spec requires the 'Joining <Room>…' text to always be the first thing visible, set roomName from a pre-flight GET /api/users/me (lastRoomId → /api/rooms/{id}) before calling autoJoin().
critical_code_review_comments: PrivateMessagesModal.tsx: handleSendMessage does optimistic-then-reload via loadMessages; consider appending the new message to local state immediately to avoid the ~2s reload flash. Non-blocking.
Backend update_dm_settings silently drops invalid allowMessagesFrom values and still returns ok=true. Acceptable as a soft contract but worth returning 422 for stricter clients.

delete_dm_conversation is a hard delete on both directions of the conversation — fine for this product but the docstring says 'hard delete is fine here as both ends share the row', though rows are NOT shared (sender/receiver inverted); harmless because the $or covers both.
test_credentials: dmtest1 / pass1234, dmtest2 / pass1234 (seeded). Tests also self-register TEST_dm_* users.
seed_data_creation: During Playwright test, 1 DM was sent from dmtest2 → dmtest1 to trigger the unread dot. Pytest creates ephemeral TEST_dm* users (left in DB; can be purged by username prefix).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter10 DM + auto-join is fully verified end-to-end on the public preview URL. The auto-join indicator can be caught with a tight polling loop (~150ms cadence) immediately after the Sign In click; it lives ~900ms total. Re-running the pytest file is safe and idempotent — it self-registers fresh users each run. Backend defaults for dm-settings are everyone/true. Frontend persists settings via PUT on every chip/switch interaction.
